title: Add color scheme options to Site Settings

requirements.summary: Update SiteSettings TypeScript type to include colorScheme field
acceptanceCriteria: SiteSettings TypeScript interface includes colorScheme: string

file_operations.description: Add colorScheme: string field to the SiteSettings interface alongside existing fields (shopifyStoreDomain, currency, address, storeName, contactEmail, shopifyStorefrontAccessToken, heroBannerImageUrl, shopifyEnabled)

requirements.summary: Add Color Scheme subsection to SiteSettingsSection component with predefined scheme selector
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Color Scheme' subsection appears in the Site Settings tab of the Admin page
At least 4 predefined color scheme options are presented as selectable swatches or radio/select inputs
Each swatch visually previews the primary colors of that scheme
The currently saved color scheme is pre-selected when the section loads
Clicking 'Save' calls updateSiteSettings with the selected colorScheme value
A success toast appears on successful save; an error toast appears on failure

file_operations.description: Add a 'Color Scheme' subsection below existing fields. Define predefined color schemes: 'default' (Ivory & Gold), 'rose-petal', 'midnight-luxe', and 'sage-cream'. Render each as a selectable swatch displaying a visual preview of its primary colors. Load the current colorScheme value from useGetSiteSettings on mount and pre-select it. Include the selected colorScheme in the updateSiteSettings mutation payload alongside existing fields. Display success/error toasts using the existing toast pattern after save completes.

requirements.summary: Apply active color scheme site-wide by injecting CSS custom property overrides on app load
acceptanceCriteria: On app load, the active colorScheme is fetched from getSiteSettings
The corresponding CSS variable overrides are applied to :root or document.documentElement
Switching the color scheme in Admin Settings and saving immediately reflects the new palette on the storefront after a page reload or query invalidation
The 'Default' scheme preserves the existing ivory/blush/gold palette with no visual regression
Each alternative scheme applies visually distinct but cohesive palette changes across the navigation, hero banner, buttons, and product cards

file_operations.description: Define CSS custom property override sets for each predefined color scheme (default, rose-petal, midnight-luxe, sage-cream) using data attribute selectors on :root (e.g., :root[data-color-scheme='rose-petal']). Each scheme overrides the brand color tokens (--ivory, --blush, --gold, --ivory-light, --gold-dark) with OKLCH values that provide visually distinct but cohesive palettes. The 'default' scheme retains the existing ivory/blush/gold tokens without changes.

Fetch the active colorScheme from getSiteSettings using useGetSiteSettings hook on app load. Apply the colorScheme value as a data-color-scheme attribute on document.documentElement (e.g., document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-color-scheme', colorScheme || 'default')). Re-apply the attribute whenever the colorScheme query data changes to support immediate reflection after Admin save and query invalidation.
